title: alsine nodosa
intro: Alsine nodosa, also known as Creeping Jenny or Moneywort, is a perennial flowering plant in the mint family native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and iridoids. The health benefits of Alsine nodosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various ailments. Alsine nodosa can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used to support liver health and digestive function.
